intro: Vandasina retusa, commonly known as the "Gentian of the Andes," is a plant species that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. Traditionally, Vandasina retusa has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Vandasina retusa include iridoids, such as gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Vandasina retusa is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and it is also used to make extracts and capsules that are used to treat digestive issues and other health conditions.
